== Notes for [[Item:Q98353|Q98353]] ==

http://www.jewishencyclopedia.com/articles/6494-gamaliel-i

Gamaliel the Elder /ɡəˈmeɪljəl/,[1] or Rabban Gamaliel I (רבן גמליאל הזקן; Greek: Γαμαλιήλ ο Πρεσβύτερος), was a leading authority in the Sanhedrin in the mid-1st century CE. He was son of Simeon ben Hillel, and grandson of the great Jewish teacher Hillel the Elder, and died twenty years before the destruction of the Second Temple in Jerusalem (70 CE). He fathered a son, whom he called Simeon, after his father, and a '''daughter, whose daughter (i.e., Gamaliel's granddaughter) married a priest named Simon ben Nathanael.''' The name Gamaliel is the Greek form of the Hebrew name meaning reward of God.

In another tradition, '''Shimon ben Nethanel married the daughter of Gamliel HaZaken.'''

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
6702EFBBE183F1EFBBF89829A6488A4D90EB
geni:6000000010971486205

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
